summary: Good‑quality recording of an Alpine Valley show where Jack Straw and Althea shine, with solid playing despite a few technical hiccups.
review: The crowd‑sourced tape captures a lively Alpine Valley performance that many fans cite as a highlight of the three‑night run – especially the fiery Jack Straw and soaring Althea.  Jerry’s lead work is repeatedly praised, while the overall set feels tight even though a few moments (e.g., The Dew) fall flat, and the recording is generally clear with only minor quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate 3‑5 stars and repeatedly praise specific songs and Jerry’s playing, while a few note the show as the weakest of the run; recording quality is described as good overall.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers call it a "good recording" and note clear sound; no specific complaints about drop‑outs or muddiness were mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds to a high‑energy Jack Straw finish; the second set includes solid numbers like Mississippi and Man Smart, but some transitions (e.g., The Dew) feel under‑whelming. Overall pacing is praised despite a few equipment hiccups.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solos are highlighted on Althea and throughout; fans note his tone (“Across the Rio Grande”) and describe him as "on fire".
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned only in passing during a backstage spat with Bob; no specific comments about his bass playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s equipment trouble is noted, along with a humorous mic intro to introduce himself.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) is referenced in the on‑stage incident but no musical commentary.
band_member_comments.Drums: One reviewer mentions being "in the drummy mood" during The Dew, indicating solid percussion work.
